product_name: Lamaze Freddie The Firefly Soft Toy
rank_rationale: #1 over #2: more grab points and a mirror.
include_rationale: Hands are aiming more now, not just brushing past toys. Freddie gives several places to grab and something to mouth once the hold sticks. Clip him where they can see him during floor play so a reach has a clear target within arm reach on the mat.

rank_rationale: #2 over #3: bigger grab target than Sophie for two-hand tries.
include_rationale: Single hand brushes are turning into proper holds. Long soft legs give a bigger target when aim is still wobbly. Keep play on the mat so a triumphant grab does not end under the sofa cushions again, and stay within arm reach the whole time.

rank_rationale: #3 over #4: classic limb grab beats ring for first holds.
include_rationale: Reaching often ends in the mouth at this age. Soft Sophie legs give something to catch and chew without hard plastic edges. Wipe clean only; she is not dishwasher gear for the steriliser cycle. Keep sessions short and stay within arm reach on the floor.

product_name: Sophie la Girafe So Pure Soft Teether
rank_rationale: #4 over #5: clearer chew-grab than a multi-piece gift pack.
include_rationale: Some babies prefer a ring they can slide and chew over a giraffe body. The textures reward a successful grab with something interesting on the gums. Keep this as the second Sophie shape, not a third Sophie buy in the same basket this month.

rank_rationale: #5: value multi-piece set; less single-toy clarity than Freddie or Sophie.
include_rationale: A single toy can bore a determined grabber. This pack spreads reaches across links, wrists and feet without buying five separate pieces. Use the linking teethers on the mat first if the soft foot pieces feel fiddly at four months old.
